name: Novada MCP
description: Remote Streamable-HTTP MCP server with 25 web-data tools — search, SERP, scrape, extract, map, crawl, verify, discover, research, and 6 proxy types. One URL, zero install, works with Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Cline, Windsurf.

offers.description: 1,000 tool calls per month free. No credit card required.

mainEntity.name: How is this different from calling the Novada REST API directly?

acceptedAnswer.text: The MCP endpoint exposes Novada tools through the Model Context Protocol so any MCP-compatible agent (Claude, Cursor, Cline, Codex…) can discover and call them with a structured contract — no client SDK, no per-tool wrapper code. Underneath, it dispatches to the same Novada backend.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a separate key for MCP?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. The same Novada API key works for the hosted MCP, the local npx novada-mcp, and the REST API. Quota counters are per token.

mainEntity.name: Why can't I use browser tools on the hosted endpoint?

acceptedAnswer.text: Headless browsers (Playwright) don't run inside serverless edge functions. The hosted endpoint returns 501 with an agent_instruction telling the agent to switch to the local MCP via npx novada-mcp — same key, same tool names.

mainEntity.name: Can I send the key as a header instead of a URL query?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY against https://mcp.novada.com/mcp works identically. Prefer the header when sharing screens or storing client configs in repos.

mainEntity.name: What's the difference between hosted and npx novada-mcp?

acceptedAnswer.text: Hosted = zero install, paste-a-URL, always latest, but no browser tools. Local npx novada-mcp = runs everything including Playwright browser flows, works offline-from-cloud, but you manage updates yourself.
